chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ أَطَاعَ الْمَخْلُوقَ فِي مَعْصِيَةِ الْخَالِقِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 161 – The one who obeys the creatures (people) regarding a disobedience to the Creator

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/69)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/69)
text: كَتَبَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى الْحُسَيْنِ ( صلوات الله عليه )  عِظْنِي بِحَرْفَيْنِ فَكَتَبَ إِلَيْهِ مَنْ حَاوَلَ أَمْراً بِمَعْصِيَةِ اللَّهِ كَانَ أَفْوَتَ لِمَا يَرْجُو وَ أَسْرَعَ لِمَجِي‏ءِ مَا يَحْذَرُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Shareef Bin Sabiq, from Al Fazl Bin Abu Qurra,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A man wrote to Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Advise me with two letters’. So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote to him: ‘The one who endeavours a matter by disobeying Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, it would be the death of what he hopes for and would hasten the coming of what he is cautious of’.
en.sarwar: 3. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Sharif ibn Sabiq from al-Fadl ibn abu Qarrah from abu Qurrah from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once, a man wrote al-Husayn (a.s.) asking, ‘Instruct me with good advice in two letters.’ The Imam wrote back to him, ‘Whoever seeks to acquire something by means of what angers Allah (his deed as such) will be most harmful to his hopes and most hastening to the coming of that which frightens him.’”
